Bhajan.title: Bhajan
Bhajan.description: Represents an individual devotional song, which can be either audio or video, including its metadata for browsing and playback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bhajan entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the bhajan, potentially AI-generated based on its title or associated keywords.

contentType.description: The type of the bhajan content (e.g., 'audio', 'video').

mediaUrl.description: The URL where the audio or video content of the bhajan is hosted.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL for a thumbnail image representing the bhajan content.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the bhajan in seconds.

artistId.description: Reference to the Artist who performed or created the bhajan. (Relationship: Artist 1:N Bhajan)

languageId.description: Reference to the Language the bhajan is primarily presented in. (Relationship: Language 1:N Bhajan)

themeIds.description: References to the devotional Themes associated with the bhajan. (Relationship: Theme N:N Bhajan)

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the bhajan was uploaded.

keywords.description: A list of keywords associated with the bhajan, useful for searching, filtering, and AI description generation.

Artist.title: Artist
Artist.description: Represents a devotional artist who performs or creates bhajans.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Artist entity.

name.description: The full name of the devotional artist.

bio.description: A brief biographical description of the artist.

profileImageUrl.description: The URL for the artist's profile image or avatar.

Language.title: Language
Language.description: Represents a language in which bhajans are available.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Language entity.

Theme.title: Theme
Theme.description: Represents a devotional theme or category under which bhajans are grouped.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Theme entity.

name.description: The name of the devotional theme or category (e.g., 'Krishna Bhajans', 'Shiv Bhajans', 'Meditation').

description.description: A brief description explaining the theme.

definition.description: Stores individual bhajan metadata and content links (audio/video). Publicly readable for browsing and playback; admin-writable for content management. Includes denormalized 'artistId', 'languageId', and 'themeIds' for efficient querying and display.

params.name: bhajanId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific bhajan.

definition.description: Stores information about devotional artists. Publicly readable for browsing; admin-writable for content management.

params.name: artistId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific artist.

definition.description: Stores definitions of languages in which bhajans are available. Publicly readable for filtering; admin-writable for content management.

params.name: languageId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific language.

definition.description: Stores definitions of devotional themes or categories. Publicly readable for filtering; admin-writable for content management.

params.name: themeId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific theme.

definition.description: Marker collection for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document here indicates that the corresponding 'userId' has administrative privileges. Used for Authorization Independence in write rules. This entity is conceptual and its schema is not explicitly provided, functioning as an administrative role marker.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of a user who is designated as an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for 'Bhajan Sangam' is designed prioritizing Authorization Independence and adherence to QAPs (Query As Predicates), ensuring a secure, scalable, and easily debuggable system. The core strategy revolves around Structural Segregation and DBAC (Database Access Control) for administrative functions.

**Authorization Independence:** All primary content collections (`/bhajans`, `/artists`, `/languages`, `/themes`) are configured for public read access. This means read operations on these documents do not rely on hierarchical dependencies or complex `get()` calls within security rules, thus ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rule evaluation. Write operations for these collections are restricted to administrators. This is achieved by checking the existence of a document in the `/admin_roles/{userId}` collection. This check is a single, direct lookup to a global role collection, which satisfies Authorization Independence as it does not create dependencies for the data itself, and is a standard pattern for DBAC.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Public Read Access:** The collections `/bhajans`, `/artists`, `/languages`, and `/themes` are designed for anonymous and authenticated public read access. This inherently supports QAPs for browsing, searching, and playback features, as all documents are readable, and `list` operations will not be filtered by security rules. Client-side queries can be applied efficiently without hitting rule-based filtering limitations.
2.  **Admin Write Access:** Administrative operations (CRUD for content entities, and management of admin roles) are protected by explicit existence checks in the `/admin_roles/{userId}` collection. This segregation means that write rules do not attempt to filter what an admin can see or modify based on content, but rather strictly enforce *who* can perform *any* write, thus adhering to QAPs for write operations as well.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   Core application data (`Bhajan`, `Artist`, `Language`, `Theme`) are each placed in their own top-level collections (e.g., `/bhajans`), ensuring uniform security posture (publicly readable, admin-writable) within each collection.
*   Administrative roles are separated into a dedicated collection (`/admin_roles`) for clear DBAC implementation.

**Access Modeling (DBAC):**
*   **Public Content:** `Bhajan`, `Artist`, `Language`, `Theme` collections allow anyone to read, list, and query, supporting core browsing and playback features.
*   **Admin-Only Write Access:** All CRUD operations on `/bhajans`, `/artists`, `/languages`, and `/themes` are reserved for authenticated users who are also designated as administrators. This is verified by checking for the existence of their `uid` in the `/admin_roles` collection.
*   **Admin Roles (`/admin_roles/{userId}`):** This collection stores documents whose mere existence signifies an administrator role for the `userId`. This follows the 'Existence over Content' principle for global roles. While `adminRole` is not explicitly defined in the provided `backend.entities`, it's a conceptual entity representing the user's administrative status, critical for implementing the required 'Admin Authentication' and 'Admin Dashboard' features securely via DBAC. Its corresponding schema reference is provided as a placeholder to meet the JSON schema structure requirements.
